parameters.content:
### Every Few Minutes

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 5 min. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Fetch Target Page

### Fetch Target Page

GET https://example-market.com/assets — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Extract Listings (CSS) · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Extract Listings (CSS)

Extracts structured values from raw HTML via CSS selectors.

Next: Rows from Arrays

### Rows from Arrays

Custom JavaScript: Zip parallel arrays into one item per listing

Next: Diff vs Last Run

### Load Previous Snapshot

googleSheets — `read` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Diff vs Last Run · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Diff vs Last Run

Diffs two streams by key into 4 lanes: only-in-A, same, different, only-in-B — the heart of the sync.

Ports: ①→ Alert New Findings · ③→ Update Changed Rows · ④→ Write Fresh Snapshot

### Alert New Findings

telegram — `action` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Merge Diff Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Update Changed Rows

googleSheets — `appendOrUpdate` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Merge Diff Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Write Fresh Snapshot

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Merge Diff Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Merge Diff Outcomes

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

### Archive Row Snapshot

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Load Previous Snapshot 2 · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Load Previous Snapshot 2

googleSheets — `read` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Fingerprint for Idempotency

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per asset (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Telegram

### Escalate Failure to Telegram

telegram — `action` operation against the connected account.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

# Alert on market moves with HTTP / REST API and Telegram and log the results

**Pattern:** Trading Alert · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Alert on market moves with HTTP / REST API and Telegram and log the results. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual price alerts is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Every Few Minutes → Fetch Target Page → Extract Listings (CSS) → Rows from Arrays → Diff vs Last Run → Alert New Findings → Merge Diff Outcomes → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to trading alert without manual effort
- Anyone automating price alerts in finance
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Standardizes the process every time
- Improves response time
- Has retries and error handling built in

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2; Telegram bot token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your price alerts process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
